highlights.text: I wrote last Monday November 24th at 4pm: “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
My general lean is to defer to the trend.
The most foundational building block of ES price action is the trap , or what I call the Failed Breakdown.
This is how institutions long: They wait for an “ elevator down” sell, wait for a low to be lost, trap shorts below the low, recover and we rally.
The squeeze is always proportionate though to the size of the elevator down sell that precedes it.

Last Tuesday at 9:40AM, we went elevator down and flushed 6701 (there was a big shelf of lows there set on Monday/early Tuesday last) to 6675, trapped shorts then recovered 6701 for a Failed Breakdown.
This can fill out then next leg sees 6863, 6877, 6889 as the first slate of targets.” However, we cannot just buy patterns - we need an entry trigger - and for that, we used Failed Breakdowns.

I also wrote on Tuesday at 4pm: “The Failed Breakdown of the 11:30AM 6818 low is also actionable.” We got exactly this yesterday morning - ES went elevator down from that 6863 flag resistance down to 6817.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into some of the Failed Breakdown’s that gave us this weeks strength (there were a multi-tude and they are great to learn from).
I wrote Tuesday at 4pm: “ES is flagging 6853 to 6805 with a 6825-30 magnet in the middle.

post_title: Another New High of Week For SPX, But Are Bulls Running Out Of Steam? Dec 5th Plan
